title: Sample A 01
wikitext:
[]: "Sample A-01"             Quote Post by Replicator » Fri Jan 18, 2013 11:26 am This is a theory/fanwank that could be another possible explanation about the nature of the Adam embryo. When the Adam embryo first appeared, Kaji says "It's restored itself this far", and the case holding Adam says "he is living" on it. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Jan 18, 2013 12:03 pm I think it's called that because the embryo was found with the organism that eventually became Kaworu. "Sample A-01" was the Adam Embryo ("A" for "Adam"), while "Sample A-02" was the Lilin organism which contained his soul, made using the DNA of the human "donor" mentioned in the Second Impact films. I assume the Evas were created from tissue samples taken from Adam before he went kablooey. Can anyone read the rest of the script on the bakelite, especially the text near the line "he is living"? Also, could this make Adam's status as male canon?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 18, 2013 2:27 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Can anyone read the rest of the script on the bakelite

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Jan 18, 2013 4:10 pm Mr. Tines wrote: "Who is man, he is ...., why?" Verb unclear; Reichu suggested "crying" some years back. The text under Caution could be blurry kanji.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Fri Jan 18, 2013 4:56 pm I can confirm that 'living' looks more likely to my eyes. I also suggest that A-01 refers to Angel-01, not Adam-01. It's interesting that the Evas grew, and yet the original body of Adam is scarcely growing, and his adorable existence is even somewhat astonishing.

[]:             Quote Post by lclbloodtransfusion » Mon Jan 21, 2013 1:43 pm who is your/Adam He is living why?

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Fri Jan 25, 2013 9:59 pm couldn't the term who is you just be something they wrote incorrectly much like a certain caution sign?

[]:             Quote Post by DeadClones » Fri Jan 25, 2013 10:42 pm This is actually the first time I noticed those scribbles were English. I can read: ADAM Who is you He is Living Why? Kinda makes me think of: "And so it is written, The first man Adam was made a living soul; the last Adam was made a quickening spirit." 1 Corinthians 15:45

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Fri Jan 25, 2013 11:55 pm Quickening?

[]:             Quote Post by DeadClones » Sat Jan 26, 2013 11:48 am sum_exsanguis wrote: Quickening?

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sat Jan 26, 2013 10:29 pm I know...isn't it also coincidentally the name of a certain 3rd movie

[]:             Quote Post by DeadClones » Mon Jan 28, 2013 11:55 pm sum_exsanguis wrote: I know...isn't it also coincidentally the name of a certain 3rd movie

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Tue Jan 29, 2013 9:18 pm I think it can fit well enough off generalizations, but that is for a spoiler post in 3.0

[]:             Quote Post by ShIiNjI » Wed Jan 30, 2013 11:12 am SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Lance of LoL » Wed Jan 30, 2013 12:09 pm I actually played a game where you raised the Adam embryo like a friggin' tamagochi. Unfortunately due to my lacking skill in Japanese I think I just fed it a few times and then completely lost track of what I was doing. Tangent aside, What I can make out of the text on the right is Caution Some things I can't read +42 and some more stuff I can't read. So either there's a Hitchhiker's reference in Eva, or there's math in dem thar frames. XD What gets my attention though is the little tag in the upper right beneath the biohazard symbol, can anyone read that? it's some word then 510 from the looks of it.

[]:             Quote Post by ShIiNjI » Wed Jan 30, 2013 1:12 pm Lance of LoL wrote: Tangent aside, What I can make out of the text on the right is Caution Some things I can't read +42 and some more stuff I can't read.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Jan 30, 2013 1:23 pm I tried putting the bottom right corner through some basic image modification options that I found on VLC, but I couldn't get it clear enough to see any distinct radicals that I could use to search for kanji. If anyone wants to give it a try, this site is a huge help.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jan 31, 2013 8:41 pm Hmmm... I'm starting to wonder if the last word is actually "why". It looks like it could be "joy".

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Feb 01, 2013 2:32 pm thewayneiac wrote: Hmmm... I'm starting to wonder if the last word is actually "why". It looks like it could be "joy".

[]:             Quote Post by BlackberryMilk » Fri Feb 01, 2013 8:54 pm slothen wrote: There's definitely an h in there.
